title: refund — retention windows
description: Refund data is financial-audit critical. Every refund is a compliance-relevant financial event — a reversal of previously-recognised revenue. Default retention 7 years (SOX + SOC 2 CC6.3 + national tax law baselines: US, EU, UK, CA). Enterprise tier extends to 10y via `refund_extended_retention`. TenantErased cascades FK CASCADE hard-delete of every owned row EXCEPT invoice-linked refunds — those migrate to a compliance archive for legal-obligation retention. Consumer-protection-law SLAs (14-30 day refund windows) do NOT affect retention — those govern how quickly refunds MUST complete, not how long records live.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) — storage limitation (retention windows justified by tax + fraud audit)
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with financial-retention carve-out per Art. 17 §3(b))
gdpr Art. 6(1)(b) — lawful basis: contract (refund = contractual reversal)
us tax law — 7-year financial-record retention (reversal accounting for revenue-recognition audit)
sox 404 — internal controls over financial reporting (reversal trail retained)
iso 27001 Annex A.12.3 — audit log retention
ccpa 1798.100 — right to know + right to delete (with retention carve-out)
ca cop 1748 — California cooling-off period refund records retained 3 years minimum
eu consumer rights directive 2011/83/EU — 14-day cooling-off records retained per member-state law (typically 5-10 years)
uk consumer rights act 2015 — records of refunds under statutory rights retained 6 years minimum
pci-dss Req 10.5.3 — transaction reversal audit trail retention 1 year online + 3 years total minimum

hot.description: Active + first year — dispute resolution + regulator inquiry response window.

purge.action: hard delete via PurgeExpiredRefundDataJob; invoice-linked refunds migrate to compliance_archive.refunds
purge.notes: Every refund traces back to a payment + invoice + credit note + transaction. Retention aligns with the longest-lived downstream — invoice/payment retention is 7y+, so refunds retention is at least 7y.

purge.trigger: co-terminous with parent refund purge (FK CASCADE)
purge.action: hard delete when parent refund purges; migrate to compliance_archive.refund_lines when parent migrates
purge.notes: Refund lines are pivot rows — retention follows the parent refund exactly.

purge.action: cascaded (JSONB column on the refund_line row)
purge.notes: The proration_config is IMMUTABLE post-refund_line creation. No PII lives in the blob (only unused_period + pass credit counts).

special_paths.action: ArchiveRefundFinancialRecordsOnTenantErasure hook migrates invoice-linked refunds (refunds where the parent invoice still exists in compliance_archive after tenant erasure) to compliance_archive.refunds + compliance_archive.refund_lines. Financial-obligation retention overrides GDPR Art. 17 §3(b) exception. Non-invoice-linked refunds skip archive.

PurgeRefundDataForErasedTenant hook FK CASCADE hard-deletes every refund + refund_line row EXCEPT the already-archived ones (from ArchiveRefundFinancialRecordsOnTenantErasure). Audit trail rows survive per audit module design (outlives-source).

special_paths.trigger: UserErased (as initiator / approver / rejector / cancelled_by / reversed_by / customer)
special_paths.action: RedactCustomerFromRefundsOnUserErased hook: schedule a 90-day-post-erasure redaction of user_id references on refunds where the user played any role. Row survives for the 7-year audit trail; the user identity is redacted to a hashed placeholder.
special_paths.carve_out: Retention windows on the refund row unchanged — financial retention overrides erasure per legal-obligation carve-out per GDPR Art. 17 §3(b) + US tax law.

special_paths.action: Retention window BEGINS at processed_at. Prior refund states (pending / awaiting_approval / approved / processing) do NOT count toward the 7y window — a refund pending for 30 days then succeeded has 7y+30d total lifetime.
Retention window BEGINS at the terminal-state timestamp (failed_at / rejected_at / cancelled_at). Terminal-state refunds are retained the full 7y for regulator + support-triage lookup.

cross_reference: audit::retention.json — audit rows outlive their source rows regardless (financial audit + tax audit trail).
activity::retention.json — activity rows follow their own tier-based retention.
finance::invoice retention — invoice-linked refunds align with invoice retention windows.
finance::payment retention — payment records must survive at least as long as their refunds.
finance::credit-note retention — credit notes issued by CreditNoteIssuer align with parent invoice retention.
finance::transaction retention — offsetting transactions align with parent refund retention (co-terminous).
